text.tspan: 图3.针对申请人的跨尺度多物理场耦合建模与材料去除机理的代表性第三方评价
The applicant's fully coupled thermo-mechanical-electrical dynamics model
provides the core theoretical underpinnings for microscopic surface properties
and offers a mechanistic explanation for the formation of dynamic errors in
Former Editor-in-Chief, Int. J. Mach. Tools Manuf.
Fellow of the Royal Academy of Engineering
Fellow of the International Academy for Production Engineering (CIRP)
In a series of research papers, the team of Prof. Di Zhu has acknowledged
the contribution of the applicant's multi-ion migration-reaction
fully coupled physical model in elucidating microscopic removal mechanisms.
The foundational nature of the applicant's work is further demonstrated
by the team of Prof. Di Zhu, who adapted the proposed ion migration model
to build a tailored simulation framework for their own research
Prof. D. Zhu: Fellow of the Chinese Academy of Engineering
Furthermore, other research groups investigating novel laser-based hybrid
processes have leveraged the fundamental principles elucidated
by the applicant's multi-physics model to inform their own mechanistic studies.
